title: Thermal Spin Polarization Driven by Nuclear Spin-Orbit Coupling in Neutron Star Pasta
authors: Hiroyuki Tajima, Yuta Sekino, Hiroshi Funaki, Shota Kisaka, Nobutoshi Yasutake, Mamoru Matsuo
abstract: We discuss anomalous spin polarization on the surface of nuclear pasta in a neutron star, driven by a nuclear spin-orbit interaction. We present an effective two-band model of surface-localized neutrons near the nuclear pasta. The central point is the emergence of a Rashba-type spin-orbit hybridization generated by the neutron--nucleus spin-orbit force in the presence of the strong density gradient normal to the pasta surface. Starting from a single-particle Hamiltonian with a central potential and a standard nuclear spin-orbit interaction, we show that the surface spin polarization occurs due to the thermal inhomogeneity even in the absence of a magnetic field. Our study links neutron-star physics and solid-state spintronics and would contribute to understanding the interplay between spin dynamics and strong magnetic fields.
summary: This paper explores how the density gradients at the surface of nuclear pasta in neutron stars create a Rashba-type spin-orbit coupling. It shows that temperature gradients can drive spin polarization via a mechanism analogous to the Rashba-Edelstein effect in spintronics. This provides a new way to understand spin dynamics and angular momentum in extreme astrophysical environments.
main_problem: Investigating how density gradients at the surface of nuclear pasta in neutron stars generate spin polarization via nuclear spin-orbit coupling, specifically through a thermal Rashba-Edelstein effect.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that a thermal gradient can induce in-plane spin polarization even in the absence of a magnetic field, establishing a link between neutron star physics and solid-state spintronics.
method: The study employs an effective two-band model, second-order perturbation theory for self-energy, and a semiclassical description of non-equilibrium distribution functions to derive Edelstein susceptibility.
model_or_system: The system consists of surface-localized neutrons near the interface of nuclear pasta in a neutron star crust, modeled using a Hamiltonian that includes kinetic energy, a mean-field potential, a Rashba-type nuclear spin-orbit interaction, and a Zeeman term.
key_observables: Surface spin polarization, spin-momentum locking, band dispersion, and Edelstein susceptibility.
important_parameters: Effective Rashba coupling strength (alpha_s), magnetic field strength (up to 10^17 G), temperature gradients, and momentum relaxation time.
main_assumptions: Single-mode approximation for surface modes, plane wave approximation for extended modes, and a Woods-Saxon-like distribution for the neutron density profile.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows a schematic of the thermal Rashba-Edelstein mechanism; Figure 2 displays band dispersion and susceptibility; Figure 4 shows a dimensionless integral related to the effective spin-orbit coupling strength.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the physical problem of neutron star pasta, develops an effective two-band Hamiltonian with spin-orbit coupling, derives the Edelstein susceptibility using semiclassical methods, and analyzes the effect of temperature and magnetic fields.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Schematic illustration of the thermal Rashba- Edelstein mechanism on a slab-pasta surface: the surface- normal density gradient ∇ρ and nuclear spin-orbit coupling generate a Rashba-like ﬁeld for surface neutrons, and an in- plane thermal drive ∇T produces a surface spin polarization.

FIG. 2. Dispersion relation Eκ(k⊥) of the surface neutrons measured from the continuum bottom of the extended modes, where we set ∆ǫ = 5 MeV, at αs = −2 MeV · fm, and ky = 0. The magnetic ﬁelds are taken as Bz = 0, 1016 G, 1017 G.

FIG. 3. Normalized Edelstein susceptibility m∗χxy/τ at the surface of nuclear pasta as a function of the temperature T , where αs = −2 MeV · fm and µ⊥= 5 MeV. The magnetic ﬁeld is taken as Bz = 0, 1016 G, 1017 G.
